answers.q1: I create a fantasy webtoon about a girl who discovers she can talk to spirits. It's serialized weekly and I have about 40K subscribers. I draw everything in Clip Studio and do my own coloring.
answers.q2: Started posting on Webtoon during college as a hobby. People liked it so I kept going. Now it's my main thing after graduating.
answers.q3: A popular creator reposted my comic and I gained 8K followers overnight. That was insane. Also finished my first year consistently updating.
answers.q4: Yeah, multiple times. Some Chinese piracy sites host my entire series. Also found someone selling prints of my character on Redbubble without any credit.
answers.q5: Devastated honestly. I spent months designing that character. Seeing someone else profit from my work felt like a violation. I cried. Then I got angry. Then I felt helpless because what can I actually do?
answers.q6: Piracy mostly. Also that I'll burn out trying to protect my work while creating it. And that platforms will change their algorithms and I'll lose everything.
answers.q7: Something that automatically finds all copies of my work across the internet and either removes them or shows me exactly where they are. Without me having to manually search.
answers.q8: Watermarks sometimes but they ruin the reading experience. I post at weird hours. I put hidden signatures in my art. That's about it honestly.
answers.q9: Maybe 200 on Clip Studio license renewal. Spent probably 20 hours total manually searching for stolen work and filing DMCA takedowns.
answers.q10: DMCA takedowns work for major platforms but not for overseas piracy sites. Watermarks work but readers hate them. Nothing really works well.
answers.q11: That sounds amazing honestly. If it actually worked and was free? I'd sign up immediately. My main concern would be if it's actually effective or just another useless tool.
answers.q12: Trust: if other artists I follow recommend it. If it has verifiable results. Suspicious: if it asks for my source files upfront. If it seems too good to be true.
answers.q13: Send me a report with links and screenshots. Give me one-click DMCA templates. Optionally auto-file takedowns on my behalf. And track which ones were successful.
answers.q14: I'd say try it if it's free. What's the worst that can happen? At least you'll know where your art is being stolen.
answers.q16: If someone I trust already uses it. If it found stolen art I didn't know about during a trial. If the interface is dead simple.
answers.q17: I just want to make comics. I don't want to be a detective. Please make this easy.

answers.q1: I do a seinen manga about a retired mercenary dealing with PTSD. Monthly updates on Tapas with bonus content on Patreon. About 60K subscribers across platforms.
answers.q2: Read manga my whole life. Started drawing seriously in college. Got into webcomics because I wanted creative control without publishers.
answers.q3: Hit 2K monthly on Patreon for the first time. Also got featured in a manga recommendation video by a big YouTuber.
answers.q4: Constantly. piracy sites, scanlation groups, someone even made a bootleg merchandise line. I've also found AI-generated art trained specifically on my characters.
answers.q5: Numb at this point. I've been dealing with this for years. Early on it was infuriating. Now it's just exhausting. I've accepted it's part of the business which is probably not healthy.
answers.q6: That AI will eventually be able to replicate my style so well that I become replaceable. That my Patreon subscribers will leave when they can get similar content for free.
answers.q7: An ecosystem where piracy is actually punished. Legal frameworks that work across countries. And AI that can't train on copyrighted work without permission.
answers.q8: I have a lawyer on retainer for serious infringement. I use TinEye reverse image searches weekly. I register copyrights formally. I monitor piracy sites.
answers.q9: 3500 on legal fees. 200 on monitoring tools. Maybe 100 hours of manual searching and enforcement.
answers.q10: Legal action works but it's expensive and slow. Monitoring tools find some things but miss others. Nothing catches everything.
answers.q11: I'd be cautiously optimistic. I've tried similar tools before and they're usually garbage. But if it actually worked at scale? Game changer.
answers.q12: Trust: transparent methodology, good track record, no BS promises. Suspicious: claiming to find everything, vague about how it works, VC-funded with no clear business model.
answers.q13: Comprehensive dashboard with analytics. Legal letter templates for different jurisdictions. Integration with platform reporting systems. Progress tracking.
answers.q14: I'd say it depends on how serious they are about their career. If they're just doing it for fun, probably not worth it. If it's their livelihood, definitely look into it.
answers.q16: Proven track record with other professional creators. Integration with my existing workflow. Reasonable pricing after free tier.
answers.q17: The free model concerns me. How do they sustain it? If they go under, I lose my protection. I need to know this is built to last.
